title: branch — field classification
description: Branch carries no PII directly. The address is business location (often published on the tenant's marketing site). Phone / email / website_url are business contacts. Coordinates are business location. Amenities + capacity are administrative. The one field with a redactor tier is `notes` (via HasMetadata → metadata jsonb) which may carry internal-only operational notes.

internal: Branch.metadata (opaque JSON operational notes bag from HasMetadata trait)
regulated_financial: Branch.name (snapshotted into historic invoices — subject to invoice retention regulations)
Branch.address (snapshotted into historic invoices for tax jurisdiction determination)
Branch.region_id (snapshotted into invoices for tax rate + currency)
audit_log: Branch.address (every change written with before/after diff)
Branch.coordinates (every change written — geofencing hand-off signal)
Branch.capacity (every change — fire-safety cap is compliance-relevant)
Branch.organization_id (immutable, but attempts to change are logged with severity=warn)

notes: Branch is one of the few tenant-scoped aggregates where NO field is confidential. The venue's address is often published on the tenant's marketing site. Phone + email are business contacts, not personal PII.

Branch.metadata is an opaque JSON bag consumers use for internal operational notes (e.g. 'key held by John, backup in front desk drawer'). Marked `internal` — never exposed on the wire by default; only surfaced when the caller explicitly requests `?include=metadata` + carries the metadata-read permission.

Branch.coordinates.accuracy_m is intentionally `internal` — knowing that a geocoder returned 20-meter accuracy vs 500-meter accuracy reveals which subprocessor the tenant uses + at what confidence, information the tenant may want to keep private.

The Branch's name + address are `regulated_financial` because Finance module invoices snapshot them at issue time. Retention windows on the audits table are set to accommodate the maximum invoice-retention regulation (7 years for enterprise tier).
Cross-tenant reads are gated by `platform.branches.viewAny` — support agents see the raw fields on the platform surface. There is no redactor for Branch fields.
